purpose: Define how Codex should choose between filesystem MCP, filesystem-admin MCP, SQLite MCP, codegraph, shell search, apply_patch, and project CLIs.

use_for: reading known small or medium text files inside allowed roots
listing known directories inside allowed roots
reading media files inside allowed roots
handling Chinese paths, spaces, and Windows quoting-sensitive paths
do_not_use_for: whole-repository code understanding when codegraph is available
large recursive text search where rg is faster and more controllable
searching broad directory trees with search_files when a known path can be read directly
manual code patches when apply_patch can provide a clearer audit trail

use_for: default bounded reads and listings through the Hub read-only allowlist
explicit full-disk or cross-directory local file inspection
system configuration or log lookup when the path is outside bounded filesystem roots
read-only diagnosis of dispersed Windows/Codex resources
filesystem-admin.write_policy: Only write through this profile after explicit user approval, backup when applicable, and a concrete target path review.
do_not_use_for: treating default profile selection as permission to write
broad recursive scans over C:\ without a narrow target, pattern, and excludes
destructive recursive delete or move operations
bypassing approval, backup, permission, or project maintenance contracts
filesystem-admin.privilege_note: This MCP exposes C:\ as an allowed root, but Windows protected-path access still depends on the Codex host process token and UAC/ACLs.

use_for: default SQLite-backed working data when the user asks for SQLite without naming a production database
agent work queues, indexes, caches, and intermediate records that are not production state
safe create/update/delete/ddl experiments inside the dedicated scratch DB
structured record writes through sqlite_insert_record, sqlite_upsert_record, or _bridge\tool_coordination.py record-* commands
sqlite-scratch.write_policy: Writable by default for scratch/work data after normal task approval boundaries; do not store secrets or production bridge state here. Complex records and UPSERT-style writes must use structured tools or coordination CLI commands instead of long raw sqlite_execute payloads.
do_not_use_for: direct mutation of bridge, mail, scheduler, Codex config, memory, or other production databases
copying secrets, tokens, private logs, or large sensitive datasets into scratch tables
using scratch data as authoritative production state
passing long raw SQL plus many parameters through Codex MCP when a structured write tool exists

use_for: read-only inspection of active mobile OpenClaw bridge tables
bridge incident analysis that needs SQL queries
counting, sampling, and joining bridge task/event state without mutating it
sqlite-bridge-ro.write_policy: Never grant write permissions to this profile. Any bridge DB mutation must go through bridge maintenance commands with backup, dry-run, validation, and explicit approval.
do_not_use_for: ad hoc UPDATE/DELETE/DDL against bridge production data

current_turn_behavior.principle: A configured and protocol-healthy MCP is not enough; use the tool only when it is exposed and callable in the current Codex turn.

filesystem_unbound.action: Use bounded CLI fallback for the current task instead of repeated tool_search loops or broad retries.
fallbacks: known text file: PowerShell Get-Content -LiteralPath with explicit path
broad search: rg or rg --files with narrow roots and excludes
generated text edit: apply_patch after approval and backup
media/file existence check: PowerShell Test-Path/Get-Item with explicit path
filesystem_unbound.closeout: Record the current-turn observation in the task summary and run mcp_session_doctor/codex_state_audit only when the task affects MCP health or tool governance.

slash_commands_unbound.action: Read _bridge/slash_commands/commands.json directly and render mentally or through the local wrapper; never treat slash commands as shell execution.
slash_commands_unbound.abort_or_hang_rule: If one current-turn slash render call aborts or hangs while direct stdio/protocol smoke is healthy, stop using the MCP tool for that task and use local registry/protocol fallback.

sqlite_unbound.action: Use _bridge\tool_coordination.py for coordination records or local Python sqlite3 only against the dedicated scratch DB; do not fallback to production DB writes.

indexed_query_contract.default_result: Return bounded decision rows with stable IDs, owner/status/time/summary fields, pagination state, and source or artifact references.
indexed_query_contract.aggregation_role: Counts, totals, rates, and health summaries supplement actual results; they must not replace representative rows required to answer the task.
indexed_query_contract.drill_down: Use a returned ID or source/artifact reference for targeted detail retrieval instead of broad log or filesystem scans.
indexed_query_contract.write_boundary: SQLite indexes remain derived query surfaces; mutations continue through the owning business or maintenance command unless the database is the dedicated scratch DB.

tool_selection.case: understand existing indexed code, call graph, or blast radius before editing
find files or text across many files
tool_selection.fallback: filesystem search only for very small, path-specific cases with explicit excludes

tool_selection.case: read a known file inside bounded roots
tool_selection.primary: Hub owner_mcp.call_readonly with filesystem-admin.read_text_file or read_media_file
tool_selection.fallback: filesystem restricted profile, then the configured MCP forward chain, then PowerShell Get-Content

tool_selection.case: filesystem MCP is configured but not exposed in the current turn
tool_selection.primary: continue from the configured profile stage to Hub fresh-stdio or same-boundary gateway
tool_selection.fallback: bounded CLI with explicit path/root only after the MCP chain reaches its local stage

tool_selection.case: read a known file outside bounded roots
write or edit source code by hand
tool_selection.fallback: filesystem edit_file only when apply_patch is unsuitable and the change is narrow

tool_selection.case: write generated non-code artifact in an allowed root
tool_selection.primary: filesystem.write_file when path and overwrite semantics are explicit
tool_selection.fallback: apply_patch for text artifacts that benefit from diff review

tool_selection.case: structured project operation with an existing CLI
tool_selection.fallback: filesystem only for inspecting its inputs/outputs

tool_selection.case: SQLite work when no production database is explicitly named
tool_selection.fallback: local Python sqlite3 against the dedicated scratch DB
bridge maintenance CLI or read-only sqlite3 query

tool_selection.case: repeatable task prompt, policy reminder, or coordination package
tool_selection.fallback: read _bridge\slash_commands\commands.json and apply the template manually

safety_rules: Before any local file modification, ask for user approval unless the current turn already clearly approved that exact change.
Before any modification, create a marked backup when the active project rules require it.
Use filesystem-admin as the default file MCP profile, while keeping every mutating tool behind explicit task authorization, backup, and owner validation.
Do not use filesystem-admin to perform broad destructive operations or broad recursive search.
For code edits, keep apply_patch as the default audit-friendly write path.
For indexed code exploration, keep codegraph as the default context source before edits.
For large searches, keep rg as the default performance path.
Treat filesystem MCP search as a bounded last resort, not a generic discovery engine.
Do not assume a configured MCP is usable in the current turn; check current tool exposure before choosing it as the active path.
If the selected filesystem MCP stage fails, continue forward through the configured MCP priority chain before using the local CLI stage.
If any MCP tool call aborts or hangs once in the current turn, do not keep probing that same tool path; classify it as current_turn_tool_surface_unstable and switch to the bounded fallback.
SQLite defaults to the writable sqlite-scratch profile for agent working data.
For complex SQLite writes, use sqlite_insert_record/sqlite_upsert_record or _bridge\tool_coordination.py record-* commands; keep sqlite_execute for short bounded SQL only.
Production or business databases must be selected explicitly and should default to read-only unless the owning maintenance command authorizes a write path.
Custom slash commands are the repeatable workflow checklist layer: render them before recurring tool-heavy flows, but never treat a template as execution or let it bypass approval and backup rules.
Use SQLite/Hub indexed query surfaces for queues, scheduler state, record-store evidence, and operational history before broad file or log scans.

Bounded or aggregate output must remain decision-complete: include representative rows, stable IDs, status/error/next action, pagination, and drill-down references; never substitute a count-only response when the task needs actual records.
After MCP config, wrapper, or tool-governance changes, validate with mcp_session_doctor smoke, slash registry validation, and codex_state_audit.

validation.slash_registry: python _bridge\mcp_session_doctor.py smoke --profile custom-slash-commands --timeout-seconds 90, plus current-turn slash_validate_registry when exposed
